heading: Add Search Nodes
body:
**Ui:**Atlas UITo configure separate Search Nodes on your Atlas cluster,
do the following:* Toggle **Multi-Cloud, Multi-Region & Workload
  Isolation (M10+ clusters)** to **On**.

* Toggle **Search nodes for workload isolation** to
  **On**.

* Specify the number of nodes to deploy. You can specify between
  a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 32 nodes.

  After deployment, you can modify the cluster to add and remove
  any additional Search Nodes.

  > **Note:** For multi-region clusters, Atlas deploys
  > the specified number of nodes in each region.
  > To learn more, see [Multi-Region and Multi-Cloud Clusters](multi-region-search-nodes).

* Expand **Cluster Tier** to select a tier for your search
  nodes in the **Search Tiers** tab.

  To learn more about the different tiers for your Search Nodes, see
  [Search Tier](select-tiers-for-search-nodes).**Atlascli:**Atlas CLITo create a search node for a cluster using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas clusters search nodes create [options]

```To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas clusters search nodes create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-clusters-search-nodes-create).**Api:**Atlas Administration API> **Note:** You can add Search Nodes to existing Atlas cluster only
> if you currently don't have any and have never created MongoDB Search
> indexes on your cluster.To configure separate Search Nodes on your Atlas cluster,
send a `POST` request to the [MongoDB Search](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-atlas-search) resource `/deployment` [endpoint](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupclustersearchdeployment). You must specify the number of
nodes and the [Search Tier](select-tiers-for-search-nodes). You can deploy between a minimum
of 2 and a maximum of 32 nodes. To learn more, see
[Create Search Nodes](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupclustersearchdeployment).When you add Search Nodes to a cluster that doesn't have any search
nodes, the existing `mongot` processes running alongside `mongod`
tail writes to the database that happen after MongoDB Search completed the
initial sync and the `mongot` processes on the new dedicated search
nodes perform an initial sync on the required collections. This
results in dual reads during the migration process.
